v_pr_key_info: Double-blind, placebo-controlled study aims to measure safety and efficacy of INDV-2000 over 3 months in participants with moderate to severe Opioid Use Disorder. INDV-2000 is an investigational non-opioid treatment that acts through the orexin pathway, shown to play a key role in the neurobiology of substance use disorder in animals. Recent animal studies have demonstrated that selective Orexin-1 receptor antagonism can reduce self-administration of opioids and synthetic opioids such as fentanyl and remifentanil. catalyst_summary: Top-line results from the Phase I proof-of-concept clinical trial of TVGN 489 were announced by Tevogen Bio on October 8, 2024, showing the therapy was well-tolerated with rapid viral elimination, no dose-limiting toxicities, and persistence through six months in high-risk COVID-19 patients.
v_pr_title: Tevogen Bio Announces Publication of Positive Phase I Clinical Trial Results for TVGN 489 in Blood Advances
v_pr_key_info: “We are excited by the outcomes of the phase I trial and are looking forward to both confirming the safety of TVGN 489 and further testing their efficacy in later stage trials in elderly and immune compromised patients with COVID-19 who continue to have higher risk outcomes,” said Dr Dolores Grosso, DNP, Tevogen’s Global Clinical Development Lead.
v_summary: Phase I clinical trial results for TVGN 489 were published in Blood Advances on June 25, 2024 (not October 8, 2024), showing 92% of patients achieved ≥88% viral elimination by Day 4 and 100% achieved >99% elimination by Day 14, with excellent safety profile and no major adverse events.
